nodes.name: Alexander Wetmore
nodes.summary: Alexander Wetmore是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联2种鸟类，包括灰鳳冠雉、Puerto Rican nightjar，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: Puerto Rican nightjar
nodes.summary: Puerto Rican nightjar（Puerto Rican nightjar，学名 Antrostomus noctitherus），分类上属于夜鷹目，并归入夜鹰科，在图谱中标记为易危（VU）物种，已关联3个分布地点节点，主要威胁包括栖息地破坏、森林砍伐、外来捕食者。

nodes.name: VU
nodes.summary: VU表示 IUCN 易危等级，当前关联211种鸟类，如丹顶鹤、索马里鸵鸟、大斑几维鸟等211种鸟类。

nodes.name: 波多黎各
nodes.summary: 波多黎各是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联5种鸟类，包括Black curassow、White-crowned pigeon、Plain pigeon等5种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 栖息地破坏
nodes.summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。

nodes.name: 森林砍伐
nodes.summary: 原生林被砍伐导致栖息地面积和连通性下降。

nodes.name: 夜鹰科
nodes.summary: 夜鹰科（Caprimulgidae）是图谱中的科级分类节点，当前关联76种鸟类，如Spotted nightjar、Solomons nightjar、Papuan nightjar等76种鸟类。

nodes.name: 夜鷹目
nodes.summary: 夜鷹目（Caprimulgiformes）是图谱中的目级分类节点，当前关联83种鸟类，如Spotted nightjar、Solomons nightjar、Papuan nightjar等83种鸟类。

nodes.name: 外来捕食者
nodes.summary: 岛屿鸟类常受猫、鼬和鼠等外来种影响。

nodes.name: Puerto Rican dry forests
nodes.summary: Puerto Rican dry forests是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联1种鸟类，包括Puerto Rican nightjar，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The Puerto Rican nightjar, Puerto Rican whip-poor-will or guabairo (Antrostomus noctitherus) is a bird in the nightjar family found in the coastal dry scrub forests in localized areas of southwestern Puerto Rico

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The Puerto Rican nightjar, Puerto Rican whip-poor-will or guabairo (Antrostomus noctitherus) is a bird in the nightjar family found in the coastal dry scrub forests in localized areas of southwestern Puerto Rico

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The Puerto Rican nightjar, Puerto Rican whip-poor-will or guabairo (Antrostomus noctitherus) is a bird in the nightjar family found in the coastal dry scrub forests in localized areas of southwestern Puerto Rico

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: The species is currently classified as Endangered due to pressures from habitat loss

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: The species is legally protected throughout its range. Puerto Rican nightjars are considered to be under pressure from habitat loss due to urban development and agricultural expansion, and through predation by introduced predators such as the small Indian mongoose and feral cats, and native predators such as owls. It is possible that the large scale deforestation that occurred during the late 1800s and the beginning of the 1900s is the reason that the nightjars are no longer found on the north part of the island. Mitigating habitat impact on private lands and controlling public access to the forest reserves during their peak nesting season has been suggested as a conservation management technique for this species

It is possible that the large scale deforestation that occurred during the late 1800s and the beginning of the 1900s is the reason that the nightjars are no longer found on the north part of the island. Mitigating habitat impact on private lands and controlling public access to the forest reserves during their peak nesting season has been suggested as a conservation management technique for this species
